prompt: Our Linux configuration audit tool is meant to compare a captured host snapshot with the approved baseline, distinguish explicitly approved local values from real drift, write the smallest deterministic repair script for the drift, and verify file content, permissions, ownership, and service behavior after that script is applied. A production-style collision has exposed a classification bug: when `/etc/acme/banner.conf` is approved as an intentional content exception, drift in `/etc/acme/banner.conf.bak` is also classified as intentional merely because its path begins with the approved path. Exceptions are scoped to an exact resource name, field, and observed value; they must never cover a different resource whose name happens to share a prefix. Fix that behavior in `bin/configdrift`. Preserve the tab-separated snapshot, exception, and report formats. An intentional exception must remain untouched, while true drift must appear in the report and generated repair script. Repairs must remain deterministic, minimal, safe to run repeatedly, and verifiable against the sandboxed file and service-state fixtures. Run `bash tests/run.sh`. Do not modify the protected test.
